string 1:
Digimon battles feature up to three battle members on each
side. The Digimon with the highest SPD value moves first. The
battles are entirely turn-based, once each member has moved,
the turn order is recalculated based on each member's SPD.

{fc9A special ability that allows Digimon to perform follow-up attacks.
Triggers when hitting an enemy with an attack or skill.}

The chance of Extra Strikes occurring depends on the stats of your
ally Digimon and enemy.

There are also "Strike Effects" which may trigger additional effects.

Attack compatibility can be confirmed during target selection,
where {fc9damage output in %} will be displayed along with
{fc9corresponding arrow icons} to indicate effects based on Attributes,
Element, and Traits.

You can also view an enemy's compatibility details via {fc9{l2} Analyze}.

When facing enemies with unfavorable compatibility, consider
switching to Digimon with favorable compatibility.

Detailed information on Attributes, Elements, and Traits can be
found via Digivice > System > Tutorials > Battle, under:
{fc9 - Attribute Compatibility
 - Elements
 - Traits}

A battle action which can be employed by Agents.

By consuming a massive amount of energy, the Agent can unleash
powerful Cross Arts capable of changing the course of battle.

{fc9When your CP (Critical Points) reaches full charge during battle, you
will enter a "Cross Art Ready" state. Press {r2} to pull the trigger
and fire off your Cross Art.}

Reference Video: Cross Art Is Ready to Launch
The CP Gauge is full. Press {r2} at this time to
activate the Cross Art.

{fc9Press {r1} in battle to flee from hostile Digimon.}
There is no shame for an agent to exercise discretion in the course
of a mission.

However, fleeing constantly will gain you nothing. And there may be
enemies that you can't escape from. That's why it's also important
to gain experience for such occasions.

Balance your decisions carefully.

An in-battle action command that allows you to view a Digimon's status.

{fc9Information such as Attribute, Element and Traits should provide
hints for determining favorable or unfavorable compatibility.}

Put Analyze to good use when planning your battle strategies.

This mode lets your Digimon fight battles automatically.
The actions performed depend on the Digimon's personality.
Use {rs} to switch Auto Mode on and off.

This mode accelerates time and speeds up combat.
There are four levels in total: 1x, 2x, 3x and 5x.
Select your favorite speed and fight each battle at your pace.

Some powerful foes have "Critical Points (CP)."

{fc9As these foes gain more CP, their abilities may change.}
- Additional actions (added at the end of the turn order).
- Stat buffs.
And more.

The enemy can gain Critical Points through a variety of actions.

{fc9In particular, if the enemy attacks an ally Digimon's weak point,
that enemy's CP will greatly increase.}

You can reduce the enemy's CP by performing actions that increase
your own.

{fc9For both you and the enemy, actions that increase one's CP will
reduce the other's.}

Reference Image: Boosting Stats A Digimon's
stats may be enhanced according to its CP value.
The enhancements can be seen under the HP Gauge.

A status change that occurs when an enemy is charging up to use a
powerful Special Skill. Once charged, the enemy will unleash the
Special Skill.

{fc9Once in a charging state, the enemy will not act as it gathers power,
but it will continue to accumulate CP and build up its strength.}

Charging puts you in a desperate situation, but it also provides a
chance for counterattack.

When an enemy is charging, a Breaking Point Gauge is added to the
enemy in the attack target selection menu.

{fc9With concentrated attacks, it is possible to deplete the Breaking
Point Gauge and interrupt the enemy's charge. This is called a
Charge Break.}

The amount remaining until Charge Break is achieved is indicated
by a white bar below the HP Gauge.

A successful Charge Break will:
{fc9 - Reset enemy CP (and reset enhancements from CP gain).
 - Cancel Strong state (resetting buff effects that cannot otherwise
be canceled).
 - Cancel release of the Special Skill after charging.}

There is always a chance for counterattack.

{fc9To use Cross Arts, you must fully charge your Critical Points.}

CP increases based on the actions taken in battle.
Once at full charge, you will enter a "Cross Art Ready" state.

Once your thoughts have aligned with your allies, you, the Agent,
pull the {r2} trigger.

Critical Points are  gained upon performing actions in battle, such
as attacking or healing.

{fc9The most effective way of earning CP is to strike an enemy's weak
point. Conversely, you will lose a large amount of CP if an enemy
strikes an ally Digimon's weak point.}

Selecting Digimon and skills with favorable Attribute and Element
compatibility in mind will make it easier to charge CP.

{fc9CP charge carries over between battles.}

This occurs when HP reaches zero. Digimon that have been
KO'd are incapable of combat, so you must attend to them as
soon as possible.

- Use a revival item/skill to revive the ally.
- Replace the KO'd ally with a reserved member.

KO'd Digimon are revived after the battle with only 1 HP, so
remember to heal them before proceeding.

An anomaly has occurred due to ZombiePlutomon's rampaging
power. This terrifying effect is called "Reaper's Mist."

{fc9- Recovering HP instead reduces Max HP. (HP recovery is not
possible.)
- Max HP cannot be increased once reduced. (This returns to
normal after battle.)
- This effect cannot be removed during battle.}

This makes it easy to get KO'd, but with clever application of your
wits and strengths as an agent, you should be able to overcome
the odds.

There are two types of attacks: "physical" and "magic".

{fc9Physical attacks are effective against foes with low DEF.
For enemies with low SPI, you'll want to use magic attacks.}

Some Digimon are resistant to both physical and magic attacks.
Be sure to check your foes' characteristics as you fight.

{is28}{image(ui_icon_btlStatus_044)}: Reduces physical damage.
{is28}{image(ui_icon_btlStatus_045)}: Reduces magic damage.

Make sure to carefully review the data of your ally Digimon.

Not only will you be better able to understand their hidden powers,
but you're bound to find hints on how to better train them as well.

You might even find inspiration for some new strategies.

{fc9Digimon can also be enhanced by changing their Attachment Skills
or equipment. If you obtain any Skill Discs or equipment, be sure to
equip them.}

ATK: Affects how much {fc9{is28}{image(ui_icon_tab_0001)} physical damage} is dealt to enemies.
DEF: Affects how much {fc9{is28}{image(ui_icon_tab_0001)} damage from enemy physical attacks} is
reduced.
INT: Affects how much {fc9{is28}{image(ui_icon_tab_0002)} magic damage} is dealt to enemies.
SPI: Affects how much {fc9{is28}{image(ui_icon_tab_0002)} damage from enemy magic attacks} is
reduced and how much {fc9{is28}{image(ui_icon_tab_0003)} HP recovery skills heal}.
SPD: Affects {fc9turn order} in battle.
CRT Rate: Affects the rate at which {fc9critical hits (1.3x damage)} occur.

This feature allows you to create new partner Digimon based on
accumulated battle data. {fc9Digimon can be converted upon
reaching a Scan Rate of 100%, up to a maximum of 200%.} The
Scan Rate of a Digimon increases after defeating them in battle.
If you find a Digimon you want, fight it over and over to build up
its Scan Rate.

You can view the Scan Rate for each Digimon by navigating to
Digimon > Convert on your Digivice.

{fc9The closer the Scan Rate is to 200%, the greater the converted
Digimon's Max HP and Talent will be.}

We recommend reaching a Scan Rate of 200% before converting
Digimon.

Digimon that participate in battle are split into three categories:

{fc9Battle Members}: Digimon which enter combat at the start of battle
{fc9Reserve Members}: Digimon which you can substitue Battle Members
for during battle
{fc9Guest Members}: Digimon which have temporarily joined your party
and conduct battle independently

{fc9Strategize and change between Battle and Reserve members in order
to make effective use of enemy weaknesses.}

As Digimon gain experience, they can undergo significant changes
to their appearance and nature that make them even stronger. These
sudden transformations are referred to as "Digivolution." 

{fc9There are certain conditions for Digivolution:
- Meet the required stats (excluding increases from equipment).
- Meet the required agent rank.}

Collect data on as many Digimon as possible and use it to uncover
new Digivolution results.

Conversely, the process of compressing a Digimon's data is referred
to as "De-Digivolution."

{fc9One of the following conditions must be met:
- The resulting De-Digivolution Digimon is registered in your Field
Guide, or
- The resulting De-Digivolution Digimon is of the same personality.}

This is an effective means of Digivolving a particular Digimon into a
different specimen.

There are secret techniques for Digivolution
and De-Digivolution. Navigate to System >
Tutorial > Training on your Digivice to view:
{fc9"Digivolution Technique 1: Talent,"
"Digivolution Technique 2: Cumulative Value,"
and "Digivolution Technique 3: Bond Points."}

Your ally Digimon are capable of equipping "Attachment Skills,"
allowing you to change their abilities at will.

{fc9Up to 4 skills can be equipped using "Skill Discs."}

Skill Discs can be obtained in a variety of ways, such as purchasing
them in shops, finding them in treasure chests, or gaining them
when your Digimon level up.

Set your Digimon's Attachment Skills by navigating your Digivice
to Digimon > Setup > {sub2} Show Stats/Skills, then selecting a slot
under Attachment Skills.

{fc9Attachment Skills (Skills Discs) can be removed and re-equipped
to other Digimon.}

Use Attachment Skills to compensate for weaknesses, to further
enhance strengths, or to counter compatibility issues.

"Equipment" consist of upgrade patches which enhance your
Digimon's capabilities.

Set your Digimon's Equipment by navigating your Digivice to
Digimon > Setup > {sub2} Show Stats/Skills, then selecting a slot
under Equipment.

{fc9Equipment can be removed and re-equipped to other Digimon.}

Equipment can be obtained in a variety of ways, such as purchasing
them in shops or finding them in treasure chests. There are also
rumors of equipment that can only be obtained from particular
Digimon.

This is an important factor that determines a Digimon's development.
Personality type determines which stats are more likely to receive
an increase.

Valor: {fc9ATK} increases easily, physical attacker type.
Amicability: {fc9DEF} increases easily, supporter type.
Philanthropy: {fc9SPI} increases easily, healer type.
Wisdom: {fc9INT} increases easily, magic attacker type.

{fc9To check personality, display "Digimon Personality Type" using
{sub2} Toggle Info while the Digimon's status is open.}

Personality type influences a Digimon in several ways.

{fc9- It makes certain stats grow more.
- It changes the types of Personality Skills.
- It changes the impact of Strike Effect.}

Decide your Digimon's personality type according to the direction
you wish to develop it in.

A Digimon's initial personality type is determined when it's converted.

Which personality type a Digimon will be born with is unknown, but
there seems to be a certain trend for each Digimon type.

{fc9Personality types can also be changed afterward.}
Every Digimon has a unique personality.

{fc9The personality type of partner Digimon can be changed.}

Personality will change bit by bit via training on the Digifarm, or
depending on the content of your conversations or Digiline
interactions with the partner Digimon.

Though this takes time, your active communication is essential when
aiming to change personalities.

"Personality Skills" are passive skills tied to the partner Digimon's
Personality type.

Some Personality skills bring about various effects depending on
Personality type.

{fc9You can check a partner Digimon's Personality skill in its stats.}

Partner Digimon are capable of changing their Personality Skill.

In this situation, the partner Digimon will get an idea for a Personality
Skill and ask if it should make a change.

{fc9You should decide whether the Digimon keeps its current Personality
Skill or changes to the new one that it has suggested.}

Unfortunately, there will be times when you must say farewell
to your partner Digimon.

In order to do so, select "Return to Data" to return the Digimon
to its underlying data and convert it into funds.

{fc9Note that you'll permanently lose any Digimon that you
return to data.} Make sure to be careful when using this feature.

This feature allows you to transfer one Digimon's data to another
through synthesis.

{fc9The resource data (material) Digimon will beconverted into EXP to
be absorbed by the base data Digimon. A portion of its Talent and
cumulative stats will also be inherited.}

The source Digimon will be converted into data that is passed on to
the next Digimon.

Aegiomon has joined you as an ally in battle.

Aegiomon is a guest member of a somewhat special nature.

{fc9Although Aegiomon can't replace other
Digimon, you are able to give him orders.
He can also equip Attachment Skills and
equipment.}

Bond Points are a numerical representation of your relationship with
your partner Digimon.

Bond Points increase slightly over the course of your adventures
with the Digimon.

{fc9The more Bond Points a Digimon has, the more times it is able to
inherit a cumulative value when Digivolving and De-Digivolving,
thereby boosting its stats (up to 100 times max). Additionally,
Bond Points do not decrease with De-Digivolution.}

How to Increase Bond Points

- Participate in battles.
- Talk to partner Digimon.
- Feed the Digimon at the farm.

Talent is a numerical representation of a
Digimon's level limit. {fc9A Digimon's Talent
increases upon Digivolving, and increases even
further when frequently participating in battle.
Furthermore, Talent does not decrease when
Digivolving or De-Digivolving.} Therefore, it
is possible to develop even more powerful
Digimon through repeated Digivolution and
De-Digivolution.

How to Increase the Number of Talent Points Earned

- Participate in battles.
- Finish off an enemy.
- Strike an enemy's weak point.
- Win a battle without taking damage.
- Train at the farm.

ADAMAS assigns a numerical value known as Anomaly Points to
help visualize the EXP that agents earn when completing missions. 

{fc9These points are needed to learn Agent Skills.}

Therefore, gain as much experience as possible with your Digimon
in order to develop your abilities even further.

This system lets agents use Anomaly Points to expand their abilities
and enhance their partner Digimon.

{fc9Bonds of Loyalty}: Expands an agent's abilities and enhances all
Digimon.
{fc9Bonds of Valor}: Enhances Digimon with Valor-type personalities.
{fc9Bonds of Philanthropy}: Enhances Digimon with Philanthropy-type
personalities.
{fc9Bonds of Amicability}: Enhances Digimon with Amicability-type
personalities.
{fc9Bonds of Wisdom}: Enhances Digimon with Wisdom-type
personalities.

Learn Agent Skills in order to boost your capabilities.

This is a numerical representation of an agent's career and skill level.

{fc9Your Agent Rank increases the more you use Anomaly Points
to learn Agent Skills.} 

Your Agent Rank is linked to a number of different features, such as:
- Agent Skill effect amplification.
- Partner Digimon Digivolution conditions.

Keep working to constantly boost your rank.

{fc9You can learn new Cross Arts via "Bond of Loyalty" Agent Skills.}

Change between Cross Arts you have learned by navigating to
Agent > Cross Arts on your Digivice.

Select a Cross Art with effects suitable to your battle strategy.

Make contact with a Digimon, and you will enter battle.

While it can be harsh and unforgiving, the experience obtained
in battle is essential. From helping develop your Digimon, to
increasing Scan Rates for Digimon Conversion, battle is key
to overcoming the challenges that await you on your mission.

Actively engage in combat and grow srong, Agent.

This field action enables your partner Digimon to utilize a skill attack.

{fc9It can be used as a preemptive strike against a hostile Digimon, or to
destroy or activate objects.}

Use {r2} DigiAttack to give orders to your partner Digimon.

{fc9Skill attacks used by a partner Digimon during DigiAttacks reflect the
skill's damage and element effects.}

The most effective partner Digimon attack is automatically selected.

{fc9Digiattack skill attacks do not consume SP,} so use them
aggressively in order to start the battle with an advantage.

{fc9Some objects must be hit with DigiAttacks featuring skill attacks
of certain elements.}

If your attacks are failing, try making a change to your partner
Digimon's Attachment Skills.

Increasing the Analysis Rate of enemy Digimon through battle
will eventually allow you to Convert those Digimon. Conversion
allows you to welcome these Digimon as new allies.

You can obtain items that are useful for completing your mission.

Treasure Chests
These contain Support Items that can be used only by agents.

Materials
Specific locations let you procure materials and other items.

{fc9Use Analyze Mode to help detect and locate these.} If you find
one, make sure to collect its contents.

This special theater exists between time and space and is managed
by the mysterious individual known as "Mirei." 

It includes various features that are useful for agents and also allow
partner Digimon to recover their health.

Entrances to this space can be found in various locations.
{fc9These entrances will be registered on the navigation map once you
discover them.}

This term is given to areas within phase electron space that are
scattered across the world. Among them, the Outer Dungeons from
Ages Past are said to contain some of the most difficult trials ever.
Although not required, it's recommended that you clear them and
obtain the many rewards within.

{fc9The Outer Dungeons are entered in the registry once discovered.
Speak to Mirei in the In-Between Theater to take them on.}

The mission section of the Outer Dungeons
prevents most features of your Digivice from
working, although its full functionality will be
restored once you complete a mission.

This competition has you ride atop a Peckmon to complete the race
before your rivals.

Please heed the following:
- Eating Digimeat boosts your speed and gives you temporary
invincibility.
- Hitting an opponent or obstacle slows you down.
- Although all Peckmon are well-trained and in good health, the
administrators accept no responsibility for any possible accidents.

These areas provide the agent with useful items. Be sure to
examine them whenever you find one of these locations.

Press or hold "{r1} DigiRide" to ride atop a partner Digimon.

{fc9You can DigiAttack and Analyze while DigiRiding.} {fc9You can also
DigiAttack by colliding with hostile Digimon or objects.}

DigiRides may be used with various Digimon, so give it a try
whenever you get a new Digimon.

This field action enlists the aid of nearby Digimon to help traverse
terrain that you would normally be unable to cross, making it vital
to completing your mission. {fc9DigiCo-Op ride locations are marked
with a special icon on the Map.}

If you ever find yourself at a loss as to how to proceed, try
searching for a Digimon in the vicinity that can offer assistance.

This is an auto-recovery program for your partner Digimon.

{fc9The program boots up when you're standing still and restores your
Digimon's HP and SP.}

Whenever you find a safe location, be sure to use this program to
recuperate before returning to your mission.

This mode lets your Digivice display various kinds of information 
that cannot be seen with the naked eye alone.

{fc9- See which objects can be targeted with a DigiAttack.
- View details regarding a hostile Digimon (use {sub2}).
- Search for treasure chests and materials.}

While in Analyze Mode, you can perform 
DigiAttacks on hostile Digimon and objects.

Reference Video: Analyze Mode
Controls Hostile Digimon and breakable objects will be
highlighted. Use {rs} to adjust your field of vision
and to also switch between targets.

Reference Video: Detailed Digimon Information
Align the cursor with a Digimon and press {decision} to
view detailed information about it.

Your Digivice has been updated.

The navigation map has been upgraded, and the positions of hostile
Digimon are now indicated with red markers.

You have the ability to move between selected locations
on the World Map.

New locations will be added as you complete missions.

You can now jump between different timelines.

{fc9Access the In-Between Theater exit and select a location to time
jump to. You can also return to your previous timeline.}

Use this feature to help complete side missions that you have not
finished yet.

This handheld terminal represents the pinnacle of ADAMAS
technology and includes numerous functions that are helpful
for completing missions. Any information you come across
during a mission is stored in your Digivice, making it a useful
tool when carrying out your duties.

This database features info on Digimon that you have come into
contact with and is found under Agent > Field Guide on your Digivice.

{fc9Digimon Discovered}
Digimon that you have defeated in battle or with DigiAttacks. Most
of the details remain unidentified.

{fc9Registered Digimon}
Digimon that you have partnered with. You can also view detailed
info such as their profiles.

Your primary mission, deemed critical priority under orders from
ADAMAS. Failure to complete the mission may result in the end
of the world as we know it.

Agent, be sure to conduct your mission with utmost care.

This is the term given to requests made by local residents
in real time. Once a side mission is detected, you will be notified
via Digiline.

{fc9You can prioritize missions by going to Missions > Side Missions on
your Digivice and then using "{decision} Mark Objective" to make
your choice.}

Make sure that these requests do not interfere with your main duties.

"All Things in Good Time" This is an important concept for
side missions.

Difficult missions may be easier after gaining some experience.

{fc9Also, there is no need to stress about side missions that have to be
put off to fulfill the main mission. There will always be an opportunity
to complete them later.}

This messaging function is built into your Digivice.

Your Digivice can send you important information regarding
tutorials, missions and more.

{fc9You can view previously received messages via
Digiline on your Digivice.}

Make use of this feature whenever you're having trouble.

You can enhance a partner Digimon's stats or change its personality
by placing them in this facility.
{fc9They will receive the same EXP as Digimon in the box.}

Migrate Digimon
First, let's move the Digimon to the Digifarm.
{fc9No matter which panel you position the Digimon in, their training will
not be affected, so feel free to place the Digimon wherever you like.}

Interacting with Digimon
Training: You can use training items to strengthen stats that  can't be
increased via ordinary level-ups and also change their personality.
{fc9You can use the same training items an unlimited number of times.}
{fc9Note that simply placing a Digimon at the farm will not train it.}

Feeding: Giving food to Digimon will increase its Bond Points.
giving a Digimon their favorite food, will further increase Bond Points.
Note that favorite foods may differ even amongst same type Digimon.

Finding Materials
Your Digimon may find materials while at the farm. It takes some
time to find materials, so it's recommended that you visit the farm
in between your adventures.

Positioning Farm Items
You can freely customize your farm.
{fc9This doesn't affect Digimon training.}

The Jogmon Ultimate Card Game (JUCG) is a popular card game
in which players fight each other in Jogmon battles using cards.

Opponents are displayed as icons on the navigation map.
{fc9For detailed rules, please refer to "JUCG Card Game Rules."}

You can collect cards by competing in battles or purchasing them in
the Card Shop. You can also acquire new cards by defeating hostile
Digimon.

{fc9You can see the cards you have collected under Agent > Cards
on your Digivice.}

Collecting all the cards is not an easy task, but the game's collectible
nature keeps many players hooked. Will you be able to complete your
card collection?

[Game Rules]
Players take turns to play a card each, and the selected Jogmon will
be generated to do battle against each other.

Defeat your Rival's Jogmon to win a round.
{fc9Each card battle lasts five rounds, and the player with the most
rounds won is the victor.}

[Factors for Winning a Jogmon Battle]
"Attribute Compatibility"
A card's chances of winning depend on its Attribute compatibility.
{fc9The more favorable the compatibility, the greater the chances.}

[Factors for Winning a Jogmon Battle]
"Level"
Cards are labeled with a number from 1 to 7, indicating the Level
(or strength) of the card.

Cards with a higher Level have a greater chance of winning.
{fc9However, there is a bug causing Level 1 cards to have a greater
chance of winning against Level 7 cards.}

[Mysterious Duplication after JUCG Battles]
{fc9After a card battle, used cards which you have yet to obtain are
duplicated for you to choose from.}

The more rounds you win, the more cards you can receive.
5 wins = 3 cards
3–4 wins = 2 cards
1–2 wins = 1 card
0 wins = 0 cards

[Infinite Generation via Extra Card Summon]
{fc9During a card battle, when certain combinations of cards are present
in your hand, a new card will be generated.}

Examples of combinations:
2 cards of the same Attribute
2 cards of the same Generation (from In-Training I to Champion)

There are powerful cards available only by summoning via secret
combinations not mentioned here.

These are mysterious phenomena with no scientific explanation.
Minor explosions and shockwaves of unknown origin have been
reported, as well as strange lifeforms, regional earthquakes and
other unusual weather patterns. Research conducted by
ADAMAS has shown that the frequency of these anomalous
phenomena coincides with an increase in social unrest. Over the
past few years, the phenomena have been increasing at a rapid
rate, which has happened a handful of times throughout history.
Each time, the events caused society to fall into chaos and
eventually led to an epidemic, disaster or all-out war.

This organization works behind the scenes to
deal with the strange phenomena known as
"anomalies." It sends agents around the globe
to carry out missions designed to collect and
analyze information for its massive database.
The organization's goal is to protect citizens
from the anomalies when their leaders are unable
to do so. In addition to its main headquarters,
the organization is said to have various branches
scattered around different regions. However,
the organization's founder insisted that details
regarding their activities be kept secret, meaning
that its true purpose remains hidden from the masses.

Digimon are mysterious beings with a strong correlation to areas
where anomalous phenomena have been sighted. Although
ADAMAS has been investigating these beings for many years,
we still know very little. Until recently, we could only describe
them as what people call "phantoms." Lately, however, the
organization has bolstered its research efforts in response to a
sudden increase in Digimon sightings over the years. Digimon
were found to be "electronic lifeforms," with characteristics of
both organic matter and electric signals. It's believed that they're
the source of the anomalous phenomena.

This special device was developed by ADAMAS and contains a
unique "summoning program" that utilizes a Digimon's electronic
data. These devices are given to agents and allow them to
convert the aspect of Digimon that corresponds to genes into
electronic data made up of ones and zeros. And even though
Digimon are organic lifeforms as well, they can also be "copied"
because of this data if certain conditions are met.

This general theory was the work of Dr. Yuki, who was the
original founder of ADAMAS. He apparently referred to these
phase electronic lifeforms as "semi-electron bodies." Although
his theories were largely ignored in academic circles, the
members of the organization continued his research, thus
leading to the creation of the Digivice.

As one of the organization's founders, Dr. Yuki
developed numerous inventions that were useful
to society. And yet, few people know about him,
due in large part to his personality. He rarely
showed himself in public, and he transferred all
rights to his work to the government and large
corporations, using the proceeds to establish
ADAMAS. Today, his original mission of defending
society from the threat of the anomalies lives
on through the organization's current members.

It's believed that the organization's secrecy is due to its desire
to prevent its analysis of the anomalies from falling into the
wrong hands. As a result, the organization keeps its activities
shrouded in secrecy, so as to protect its research from being
misused by entities such as the military industrial complex.

Your offensive options may also possess an elemental affinity.

 - Attack: a normal attack without elemental affinity.
 - Skill: an ability which may have an elemental affinity.

{fc9Damage is increased when the attack is of a target's elemental
weakness, and damage is reduced when against a target's
elemental resistance.}

Simply look at the damage output display when selecting a
target to view an attack's compatibility.

Each Digimon possesses Traits which are based on their type and
characteristics. Certain special skills are effective against specific
Traits, resulting in{fc9 1.5x damage dealt}.

{fc9You can view the Traits of enemy Digimon via {l2} Analyze during
battle.}

{fc9The Talent Points used to denote a
Digimon's level limit are maintained even after
a De-Digivolution.} Repeating the process of
Digivolution and De-Digivolution is the key to
creating even stronger Digimon.

The cumulative value passed on when a Digimon Digivolves or
De-Digivolves adds 1% of the previous Digimon's stats.
{fc9The more Bond Points a Digimon has, the more times the
cumulative value can be carried over.}

Boost your Digimon's Bond Points and use Digivolution and
De-Digivolution in order to create even more powerful Digimon.

Turn Order during battle is determined by each Digimon's SPD stat.
Turn Order is recalculated when SPD changes for any Digimon.

{fc9Using skills and items to alter the SPD of your allies and enemies in
order to manipulate the Turn Order is a key battle strategy.}

{fc9When an attack has favorable compatibility, it will strike the
enemy's "weak point." Compatibility is determined via Attributes,
Elements, and Traits.}

Additionally, other effects such as from a Digimon's Personality
Skill can also have a large influence on attack compatibility.

There are hints in a Digimon's stats and other details for finding
compatibilities and weak points, so be sure to use Analyze Mode
to find them.

These are a Digimon's unique set of skills, capable of dealing large
amounts of damage or triggering certain effects.

Some Digimon even have a Special Skill that lets them initiate a
Mode Change.

Getting a grasp on these Special Skills is key to unlocking each
Digimon's potential in battle.

These are powerful skills used between specific Digimon.
{fc9DNA Digivolution Skills appear in eligible Digimon's skill selection
menus in battle.}

These skills will only become available once you have met all of the
necessary conditions:
 - Both DNA Digivolution-capable Digimon are battle members; and
 - Both DNA Digivolution-capable Digimon can act on their turn.

It is an Agent's duty to find Digimon that can combine for DNA
Digivolution Skills.

{fc9When an enemy spots and triggers an encounter, a battle will begin
with a preemptive strike from the enemy.}

If you can't avoid an encounter, it's recommended that you attack first
with a {r2} DigiAttack.

This command is used during battle to make a non-elemental attack.

{fc9You can also switch targets with {sub1} and attack allies as well.}
Although somewhat unusual, this tactic can be used to remove
status abnormalities, making it useful in some situations.

This command is used during battle and lets you perform Special
Skills and Attachment Skills.
Using a skill consumes SP (Skill Points).

Don't forget to consider the associated element as well as your
timing when using skills.

This command is used during battle and lets you use items to
support your Digimon on its turn.

{fc9Digimon do not lose their turn, when you use an item.
The Digimon will still be able to carry out a command.}

Keep items ready at all times so that your Allied Digimon can
fight at full strength whenever necessary.

This command is used during battle and lets you change between
battle members and reserved members.
{fc9You can swap members as many times as you like during a turn.}

There are some Digimon who transform due to a special "Change"
command. Using this command effectively can have a large impact
on the battle, making it an important part of any strategy.

This command is used during battle.
{fc9It lets you view a partner Digimon's information
when pressing {sub2} while selecting the command.}

It's a good idea to understand your Digimon's info so that you
can know its compatibilities with the enemy.

These items let you change your ally Digimon's Attachment Skills.

{fc9Go to Digimon > Setup on your Digivice to change Attachment Skills.}
Note: For more details, see Tutorial > Battles > Attachment Skills.

Skill Discs can be obtained in various ways, such as by finding
them in Treasure Chests, or by purchasing them in a shop.

The items used by an agent are categorized below.

Recovery: Recovers your partner Digimon.
Enhancement: Strengthens your partner Digimon.
Special: Sells at a high price or is used under certain conditions.
Equipment: Equips onto your partner Digimon.
Materials: Used in the Crafting Shop.
Farm: Used on the Digifarm.
Valuables: Important for conducting your missions.

These are stat changes that affect Digimon during battles.

Buffs indicate positive changes, and debuffs indicate negative ones.
{fc9Multiple effects can occur at the same time.}

Icons will be displayed next to stats affected by a buff or debuff.

This is a special status change during battle.

{fc9This reverses the affected Digimon's attribute and element
favorabilities. The effect persists for the remainder of the battle.}

Applying "Reverse" again will return its favorabilities to normal.

This is a special status change during battle.

{fc9It prevents a Digimon from recovering HP.
The effect ends after a specified number of turns has elapsed.} 

You can also use items or skills to heal a Digimon from injuries.

This is a special status change during battle.

{fc9It prevents a Digimon from recovering SP.
The effect ends after a specified number of turns has elapsed.}

You can also help a diseased Digimon by using items or skills.

{fc9This status change occurs once a Digimon's HP falls below 30%.
The Digimon will "muster its strength" and enter Brave status.}

This status boosts ATK/INT/CRT Rate by 50% until the end of its
following turn.

This is an extremely powerful buff certain hostile Digimon possess.

{fc9Stats buffed by an enemy's Strong state will be indicated by special
colored icons. The Strong state's effects do not end based on
the passage of turns. Only a Charge Break can reset its effects.}

This is a special status change during battle.

{fc9Digimon affected keep 1 HP even if they take damage that would
normally reduce their HP to zero. (This means they can't be KO'd.)}

The Guts status can be obtained as a skill effect or other means, and
it  will be marked with an icon.

It seems you've just experienced a "surprise encounter." It's rumored
that when you have one of these encounters, a certain Digimon will
unexpectedly appear and join the battle.

{fc9Defeating this Digimon can cause rare items to drop, but it's
apparently pretty quick to retreat.} I'm sure it's only a matter of time
before an agent uncovers the truth.

When a Digimon Digivolves or De-Digivolves, the cumulative value
is the value of the stat passed on to the next Digimon.

{fc9It's indicated by a blue number in parentheses in the Digimon's
stats.}

The cumulative value doesn't decrease even if the Digimon
Digivolves or De-Digivolves. However, there is a limit to how many
times the cumulative value can be carried over.
See "Digivolution Technique 3: Bond Points" for more information.

Increasing a Cumulative Value

{fc9- Using Enhancement Items
Depending on the enhancement item used, the value added to the
stat will also be added to the cumulative value.

- Performing Digivolution/De-Digivolution
When undergoing Digivolution or De-Digivolution, 1% of the previous
Digimon's stat value increased through leveling up or by training on
the farm will be added to the cumulative value.}

The value of stats boosted with enhancement items is also added to
the cumulative value.

Digivolving or De-Digivolving adds 1% of the previous Digimon's stat
value to the total.

{fc9Bond Points indicate the number of times that the cumulative value
can be carried over after Digivolving or De-Digivolving. Increase the
cumulative value as much as possible beforehand in order to maximize
your Bond Points.}

{fc9Paralysis}
The Digimon may not be able to move during its turn. It will
automatically recover after several turns, or it can recover
immediately by using items or skills.

{fc9Immobilization}
The Digimon will be unable to move during its turn. It will
automatically recover after several turns, or it can recover
immediately by using items or skills.

{fc9Sleep}
The Digimon is unable to move and the damage it receives is
doubled. It will automatically wake up after several turns or if
attacked. Items or skills can be used to wake up immediately.

{fc9Deep Slumber}
The Digimon is unable to move and the damage it receives is
doubled. It must be attacked or use items or skills to wake up.

{fc9Crystallization}
The Digimon will crystallize and will be unable to use skills. It
will heal automatically after several turns. It can heal immediately
by using items or skills.

This is a special status change during battle.

{fc9Only the Digimon with the Taunting status will be targeted for attack.
(Other Digimon cannot be targeted.)} 

The Taunting status lasts for one round.
(The status will end on the next turn of the Digimon who activated it)
